title: Student Assistant (Hilfskraft)
points: Grading weekly exercise sheets for the Numerical Optimization lecture (Prof. Dr. Volker Schulz) during the summer semester 2026.
Reviewing student work on unconstrained and constrained optimisation, line-search and trust-region methods, KKT conditions, and numerical linear algebra for optimisation, with written feedback.

title: Software Developer (SDE II)
points: Designed a rule-based, declarative content-matching framework for tax-document field comparison over SAP iFlow, letting country teams configure field comparisons with no custom code. Cut new-country onboarding from about 10 days to about 3 (~70%); adopted by the Spain, India, and Chile teams.

Built an iFlow bulk-retrieval algorithm on the Document Consistency Cockpit (DCC) Spain reporting path, replacing about 100 paginated API calls with a single bulk request and cutting report generation time by about 30%.

Ideated a per-customer self-correcting ML system for content-matching result processing, designed for in-tenant training to respect GDPR; presented at an internal SAP innovation forum and accepted as a future direction.
Promoted from SDE I to SDE II on consistent top reviews and selected for SAP Labs India's fast-track architect career-development program, the only person from the area on it at the time.

title: Associate Software Developer (SDE I)
points: Owned the result-processing pillar of the Document Consistency Cockpit (DCC) end-to-end: architected the schema, decoupling layer, factory-based extensibility for new document types and countries, idempotency, and retry semantics. Load-tested at 10M to 100M document cycles; deployed for Spain and India.

Initiated and led a Turkey error-handling overhaul on SAP DRC, categorising two years of incidents by root cause and shipping fixes plus clearer messages and FAQs. Incident volume fell about 88% (roughly 50 to 2-3 per month) and the support rating rose from 2-3 to 4-5 (~66%).

Re-engineered a failing daily Turkey vendor reconciliation job, replacing a timing-out database join with a hash-map lookup keyed on tax ID and eliminating recurring failures as the ~10M-entry registry grew.

Architected Data Protection and Privacy (DPP / GDPR) compliance across the SAP Document Consistency solution and delivered legal and feature updates across Turkey, Mexico, and Chile. Recognised with the SAP Stellar Award (2023) for the DCC and Turkey work.

title: Software Engineering Intern
points: Shipped backlog features on the SAP eDocument solution (now SAP DRC), including Turkey delivery-note support and an XML proxy update for upstream format changes.
Delivered consistently ahead of deadline with near-zero QA defects, earning a direct full-time offer on the same team.
